nodes.name: HMC
nodes.description: Ingested pipeline from HMC

nodes.name: InitializeSamplerAndRNGState
nodes.description: Construct immutable HMC sampler state and initialize explicit RNG/PRNGKey state.

inputs.name: seed
inputs.constraints: used to create RNG state / PRNGKey

outputs.name: hmc_state
outputs.type_desc: StateModelSpec{position, logp_current, gradient, step_size, n_leapfrog, mass_matrix}

nodes.type_signature: (target_log_prob_fn: callable(position) -> log_prob and/or gradient, initial_positions: vector or tensor, step_size: float, n_leapfrog: int, seed: int) -> tuple[StateModelSpec{position, logp_current, gradient, step_size, n_leapfrog, mass_matrix}, RNG state or jax.random.PRNGKey, pure log-probability/gradient evaluator]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Immutable Stochastic State Bootstrap. Creates an initial immutable state for a gradient-informed stochastic transition system from a numeric starting tensor and fixed hyperparameters. It evaluates a pure scalar-field oracle (and optional derivative) at the initial tensor to cache local energy and slope information, then packages these with integration controls and metric structure into a state record. In parallel, it deterministically maps an integer seed to an explicit pseudorandom state token that must be threaded through future transitions.. Applications: Initializing stochastic trajectory proposals in robot motion planning over continuous configuration spaces, Bootstrapping gradient-based random exploration of molecular conformation landscapes in computational chemistry, Seeding uncertainty-aware parameter-space exploration for geophysical inverse models, Starting constrained stochastic search in high-dimensional engineering design optimization

nodes.name: IntegrateHamiltonianProposal
nodes.description: Generate deterministic HMC proposal by leapfrog integration over position and momenta using mass matrix and log-probability gradients.

inputs.name: state_in
inputs.type_desc: StateModelSpec{position, momenta, gradient, step_size, n_leapfrog, mass_matrix}

nodes.type_signature: (state_in: StateModelSpec{position, momenta, gradient, step_size, n_leapfrog, mass_matrix}, log_prob_oracle: callable(position) -> log_prob and gradient) -> StateModelSpec{proposed_position, proposed_momenta, proposed_logp, proposed_gradient}
nodes.conceptual_summary: Symplectic Gradient Flow Proposal Integrator. Transforms an initial augmented state \((x, v, \nabla f(x))\) into a new augmented state by alternately applying half-step updates to the auxiliary vector and full-step updates to the primary vector under a positive-definite metric tensor. The transform performs a fixed number of explicit, gradient-driven leapfrog steps using oracle evaluations of a scalar potential and its gradient, yielding a deterministic proposal map. The resulting map approximates continuous Hamiltonian flow while preserving phase-space volume and reversibility structure.. Applications: Posterior exploration in PDE-constrained inverse problems (e.g., recovering latent spatial fields from indirect measurements), Sampling high-dimensional latent trajectories in systems biology state-space models, Uncertainty quantification for calibrated parameters in climate or fluid simulation surrogates, Probabilistic inference over robot morphology/control parameters in model-based design spaces

nodes.name: IntegrateLeapfrogDynamics
nodes.description: Run the leapfrog recurrence for `n_leapfrog` steps using kick-drift-kick updates: AD-enable position, apply half-step momentum update from cached gradient summands, drift position by full step, evaluate log-probability gradient at new position, apply second half-step momentum update, and carry forward the new gradient summands.

inputs.name: n_leapfrog
inputs.constraints: Number of leapfrog steps; may be zero.

nodes.type_signature: (initial_position: Tensor<B, 2>, initial_momenta: Tensor<B, 2>, initial_grad_summands: Tensor<B, 2>, step_size: T, n_leapfrog: usize, log_prob_oracle: callable(position) -> {log_prob: Tensor<B,1>, gradient: Tensor<B,2>}) -> tuple[Tensor<B, 2>, Tensor<B, 2>, Tensor<B, 2>]

nodes.name: EvaluateProposalLogProbability
nodes.description: Evaluate target log-probability at the integrated position.
nodes.type_signature: (integrated_position: Tensor<B, 2>, log_prob_oracle: callable(position) -> {log_prob: Tensor<B,1>, gradient: Tensor<B,2>}) -> Tensor<B, 1>

nodes.name: RecoverProposalGradient
nodes.description: Convert scaled gradient summands back to raw gradient at the proposal position.

outputs.name: proposed_gradient
outputs.constraints: Gradient of log-probability at integrated position.

nodes.type_signature: (final_grad_summands: Tensor<B, 2>, step_size: T) -> Tensor<B, 2>

nodes.name: AssembleDetachedProposalState
nodes.description: Detach integrated tensors from AD graph and assemble final proposal state fields.
nodes.type_signature: (integrated_position: Tensor<B, 2>, integrated_momenta: Tensor<B, 2>, proposed_logp: Tensor<B, 1>, proposed_gradient: Tensor<B, 2>) -> tuple[Tensor<B, 2>, Tensor<B, 2>, Tensor<B, 1>, Tensor<B, 2>]

nodes.name: ApplyHMCAcceptRejectKernel
nodes.description: Perform one HMC transition kernel step: consume current state, evaluate proposal, and return accepted/rejected next state with updated gradient.
nodes.type_signature: (state_in: StateModelSpec{position, logp_current, gradient, mass_matrix}, proposal_state: StateModelSpec{proposed_position, proposed_momenta, proposed_logp, proposed_gradient}, rng_state_in: RNG state or jax.random.PRNGKey) -> tuple[StateModelSpec{position, logp_current, gradient, mass_matrix}, RNG state or jax.random.PRNGKey, TraceEvent{accept_prob, accepted}]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Energy-Consistent Stochastic Proposal Filter. Consumes a current immutable state tuple and a deterministic candidate tuple, then performs a probabilistic accept/reject gate using an energy-difference-derived acceptance probability. If accepted, the candidate position and local derivative information become the next state; otherwise the prior state is retained. This creates a one-step Markov transition that corrects proposal bias while threading and advancing explicit random-state input.. Applications: Uncertainty-aware parameter estimation in nonlinear PDE-constrained inverse problems (e.g., geophysical field reconstruction), Conformation-space exploration for high-dimensional molecular or materials simulations, Posterior sampling in medical image reconstruction with physics-based forward operators, Probabilistic calibration of complex agent-based or climate-scale simulation models

nodes.name: DeriveChainDimensions
nodes.description: Extract chain count and latent dimensionality from the current position tensor shape.
nodes.type_signature: (state_position: Tensor[B,2]) -> tuple[usize, usize]

nodes.name: SampleInitialMomenta
nodes.description: Sample initial Gaussian momenta for each chain and advance tracked RNG state.
nodes.type_signature: (n_chains: usize, dim: usize, rng_state_in: RNG state or PRNGKey) -> tuple[Tensor[B,2], RNG state or PRNGKey]

nodes.name: EvaluateCurrentLogDensityAndHalfStepGradient
nodes.description: Evaluate current batch log-density and its gradient, then scale gradient for leapfrog half-step momentum update.
nodes.type_signature: (state_position: Tensor[B,2], target_logp_fn: fn(Tensor[B,2]) -> Tensor[B,1], step_size: scalar T) -> tuple[Tensor[B,1], Tensor[B,2], Tensor[B,2]]

nodes.name: ComputeCurrentHamiltonian
nodes.description: Compute current kinetic energy and Hamiltonian from initial momentum and current log-density.
nodes.type_signature: (momentum_0: Tensor[B,2], logp_current: Tensor[B,1]) -> tuple[Tensor[B,1], Tensor[B,1]]

nodes.name: IntegrateLeapfrogProposal
nodes.description: Run leapfrog dynamics to produce proposed position, momentum, and target log-density (and proposal gradient if available).

outputs.name: proposed_gradient
outputs.constraints: shape=[n_chains, dim], if computed by integrator

nodes.type_signature: (state_position: Tensor[B,2], momentum_0: Tensor[B,2], half_step_grad_summands: Tensor[B,2], mass_matrix: mass matrix descriptor, target_logp_fn: fn(Tensor[B,2]) -> Tensor[B,1]) -> tuple[Tensor[B,2], Tensor[B,2], Tensor[B,1], Tensor[B,2]]

nodes.name: ComputeLogAcceptanceAndProbability
nodes.description: Compute proposed Hamiltonian, log acceptance ratio, and acceptance probability per chain.
nodes.type_signature: (h_current: Tensor[B,1], proposed_momenta: Tensor[B,2], proposed_logp: Tensor[B,1]) -> tuple[Tensor[B,1], Tensor[B,1], Tensor[B,1], Tensor[B,1]]

nodes.name: AdvanceHostRngStatePerChain
nodes.description: Advance host RNG by drawing one scalar per chain (matching the explicit loop side-effect), without using drawn values.
nodes.type_signature: (rng_state_after_momentum: RNG state or PRNGKey, n_chains: usize) -> RNG state or PRNGKey

nodes.name: DrawUniformThresholdsAndAcceptanceMask
nodes.description: Sample uniform thresholds for each chain, compare in log-space with accept_logp, and broadcast acceptance mask across dimensions.
nodes.type_signature: (n_chains: usize, dim: usize, accept_logp: Tensor[B,1]) -> tuple[Tensor[B,1], Tensor[B,1], Tensor[B,1,Bool], Tensor[B,2,Bool]]

nodes.name: AssembleNextStateAndTransitionStats
nodes.description: Select accepted/rejected chain-wise fields to form next state, propagate RNG state, and emit transition statistics.

outputs.name: state_out
outputs.constraints: accepted chains take proposal; rejected chains keep current

nodes.type_signature: (state_position: Tensor[B,2], logp_current: Tensor[B,1], gradient_current: Tensor[B,2], mass_matrix: mass matrix descriptor, proposed_position: Tensor[B,2], proposed_logp: Tensor[B,1], proposed_gradient: Tensor[B,2], accepted: Tensor[B,1,Bool], accepted_broadcast: Tensor[B,2,Bool], accept_prob: Tensor[B,1], rng_state_after_host_draw: RNG state or PRNGKey) -> tuple[StateModelSpec{position, logp_current, gradient, mass_matrix}, RNG state or PRNGKey, TraceEvent{accept_prob, accepted}]

nodes.name: CollectPosteriorSamples
nodes.description: Run burn-in and collection loops, repeatedly applying the HMC kernel and accumulating sample trace.

inputs.name: state_in
inputs.constraints: immutable chain state at loop start

nodes.type_signature: (state_in: StateModelSpec, rng_state_in: RNG state or jax.random.PRNGKey, n_collect: int, n_discard: int) -> tuple[Trace{samples, log_probs, acceptance_stats}, StateModelSpec, RNG state or jax.random.PRNGKey]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Two-Phase Markov Trace Collector. Iteratively applies a stochastic state-transition operator to an immutable chain state while threading an explicit random-state object through each step. The first phase executes a configurable number of transient iterations without retention to reduce initialization bias in downstream summaries. The second phase executes additional iterations and immutably accumulates selected per-step observables into a structured trace, then returns the terminal state and terminal random state.. Applications: Computational chemistry: generate equilibrium conformational ensembles of molecular systems after discarding initial transient dynamics., Robotics and control: sample latent trajectory hypotheses in a probabilistic state-estimation pipeline while logging acceptance and score diagnostics., Geophysics: explore high-dimensional subsurface parameter fields with transition diagnostics for uncertainty quantification., Systems biology: sample kinetic-parameter configurations for dynamical network models while separating warm-up and retained draws.

nodes.name: InitializeCollectionLayout
nodes.description: Infer chain and latent dimensions from the incoming state and define the collection buffer layout for n_collect draws.

inputs.name: state_in
inputs.constraints: Must contain current positions with shape [n_chains, dim]

outputs.name: trace_spec
outputs.constraints: Defines collection-major sample layout [n_collect, n_chains, dim]

nodes.type_signature: (state_in: StateModelSpec, n_collect: int) -> TraceLayoutSpec{n_collect, n_chains, dim}

nodes.name: RunBurnInTransitions
nodes.description: Advance the Markov state through n_discard burn-in transitions without recording samples.
nodes.type_signature: (state_in: StateModelSpec, rng_state_in: RNG state or jax.random.PRNGKey, n_discard: int) -> tuple[StateModelSpec, RNG state or jax.random.PRNGKey]

nodes.name: RollOutSamplingTrajectory
nodes.description: Run n_collect HMC transitions and accumulate per-step samples, log-probabilities, and acceptance statistics in collection order.

outputs.name: samples_collect_major
outputs.constraints: One state snapshot per collection step

nodes.type_signature: (state_start: StateModelSpec, rng_state_start: RNG state or jax.random.PRNGKey, n_collect: int, trace_spec: TraceLayoutSpec{n_collect, n_chains, dim}) -> tuple[Tensor[n_collect, n_chains, dim], Tensor[n_collect, n_chains] or equivalent, AcceptanceTrace, StateModelSpec, RNG state or jax.random.PRNGKey]

nodes.name: CanonicalizeSampleAxisOrder
nodes.description: Convert collected sample tensor from collection-major order to chain-major order expected by downstream consumers.
nodes.type_signature: (samples_collect_major: Tensor[n_collect, n_chains, dim]) -> Tensor[n_chains, n_collect, dim]

nodes.name: AssemblePosteriorTraceAndTerminalState
nodes.description: Package reordered samples with log-probability and acceptance traces into the final trace object and expose terminal state outputs.
nodes.type_signature: (samples_chain_major: Tensor[n_chains, n_collect, dim], log_probs: Tensor[n_collect, n_chains] or equivalent, acceptance_stats: AcceptanceTrace, final_state: StateModelSpec, final_rng_state: RNG state or jax.random.PRNGKey) -> tuple[Trace{samples, log_probs, acceptance_stats}, StateModelSpec, RNG state or jax.random.PRNGKey]
